verbatim_text: And that, when applied to the input, gives you the final set of decoded tokens, which is F of X. At least that's how I think about it. Awesome. So yes, so we have sort of answered the first part, which is parametrization. LLM scan reps and algorithms and test time adaptation is just sampling more tokens. What about the objective? And note that we just looked at this notion of terminal rewards. So it's kind of natural to try and maximize this notion of terminal rewards to learn better algorithms. And here I'll talk about a line of work that uses standard reinforcement learning to maximize these terminal rewards and learn better LLM. And I'll also talk about one of my works that says why this is not enough to learn algorithms. So first step is to define the objective. How do we compute it? You have this data set of training problems. You feed each problem as an input to the LLM.
